title: triticum sativum subsp. turgidum
intro: Triticum sativum subsp. turgidum, commonly known as durum wheat, is a type of hexaploid wheat plant belonging to the Poaceae family. The medicinal properties of durum wheat include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion due to its high fiber content and presence of antioxidants. The health benefits of consuming durum wheat include lowering cholesterol levels, regulating blood sugar, and providing essential nutrients such as iron, zinc, and B vitamins. Durum wheat can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and flour, which can be consumed as a dietary supplement to promote overall health and well-being.
